item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of my favorite skips fall into this category: the leftover scene of a sad little mystery. This is nicely done, and caters directly to my interests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has nothing to do with werewolves. It's referring to poisoning the entity within SCP-5256.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t know, I assumed that the bars were noted to indicate whoever used to live in that room was a prisoner and from the rest of the article that is logical

Anyway, good shit +1

Aconite made me think we were talking about werewolves tho… am I missing something about that chemical?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your feedback. I've removed that part of the footnote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For reasons currently unknown, the previously stated window had metal bars installed on its exterior when it was first discovered. These modifications have since been removed.

I detest this footnote. The Foundation removing part of a building with highly anomalous stuff going on is absurd. I thought this would be a Chekov's Gun moment, where we call this back later. We do not. I get the meaning behind what it was there for, but for the Foundation to go "Huh, looks like these iron bars were meant to contain something, maybe secure it and protect people from whatever's inside. Ah fuck it, gimme my sledge, that's gotta go, it's hell on the property value of the place."

The rest of this is good. But the above is a big stumbling block, nearly in itself enough to keep me from a +1, but not quite.
I sadly also had to read the spoiler to understand what was going on but now that I go back over the document, it makes much more sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for bringing it to my attention. It's fixed now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure Goldfish Crackers are capitalized Goldfish, not GoldFish, unless I'm missing something.

item.title: Re: good stuff
item.content:encoded: I think you may be confused by the term "stakes". I'm referring to wooden stakes like this.

item.title: good stuff
item.content:encoded:
I am terrible at solving mysteries, puzzles, etc so I looked at the spoiler. Good article though, I enjoyed reading it. +1 from me.

some nitpicks
whoosh

Due to the lack of knowledge regarding SCP-5256's true nature, entry to the anomaly is limited — only being permissible per a formal request to the current acting research head of SCP-5256.

Stylistic choice maybe, but the em dash isn't necessary here. Put a comma instead, like this:

Due to the lack of knowledge regarding SCP-5256's true nature, entry to the anomaly is limited, and it is only permissible per a formal request to the current acting research head of SCP-5256.

Only a single book pertaining to ceasing witchcraft and exorcisms being deemed of any interest.

Weird wording here. maybe this will fix:

Only a single book pertaining to "ceasing witchcraft" and exorcisms deemed interest.

This mixture along with stomach acid is also present in small amounts within the air inside SCP-5256.

Needs some commas, also there's too many preposition phrases in a row:

This mixture, alone with stomach acid, is present in small amounts in SCP-5256's atmosphere.

A wooden crucifix along with stakes made of the same material.

Along what though? the wall? And just saying "same material" doesn't mean the reader will assume it's the Aconite mixture. Fix:

A wooden crucifix with stakes made of the Aconite mixture.

A plastic syringe containing medical-grade anesthetics containing trace amounts of neural tissue which possessed low Hume levels.

Word choice fix:

A plastic syringe containing medical-grade anesthetics with trace amounts of neural tissue, which registered low Hume levels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You make a fair point. I'll try to make the story a bit more noticeable. Thank you for your feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tentative upvote. I shouldn't have to read the discussion page to fully grasp what's happening here, but upon further inspection, you did leave clues — albeit subtle ones — in the article.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the upvote and I'm glad you liked it. As for your spoiler, you're correct.

I think this is like the fourth antimemetic corpse we have (one of which was written by me)…

Also holy shit. That second to last line might be one of the most horrifying things on this website.

spoiler?
spoiler?

They crucified a reality bender.

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
It really is quite strange how humans will act even towards the ones closest to them with such anger when faced with something they don’t understand.


Hi everyone, I would like to give a special thank you to Tanhony, DrAkimoto, Dr Moned, DarnellJermaine, Meska, and Veralta for their help in critiquing the drafts of this article.
◎ Spoilers ◎
◉ Close ◉

Okay, so after reading this — you may be a bit confused. So first off, the thing in the corner of the room is the dead body of a reality-bender; the boy in the window — to be exact and the "metallic objects" are nails. The reason they died was that his parents were deeply religious and in them not understanding their son's anomalous properties, they believed him to be demonically possessed and as stuff like exorcisms didn't work, they resorted to killing him to free his spirit in a way.

All of the weird things in the room are the process of the boy being killed with poison, weapons, and religious objects. PoI-5256 was a friend of the boy who witnessed the murder in the last time she saw him. With the boy not wanting the girl to have to live with the knowledge, he gave himself an anti-memetic filter to remove all knowledge of him. The term "impurities" refers to what they call anomalies in general.

With all of this weird stuff happening, the parents fled and are currently being searched for by the Foundation.
